items.item: Mobile players currently skip the first-run VaultFront tutorial entirely because the modal is suppressed on narrow screens. Replace the silent suppression with a compact bottom strip that teaches the same progression without blocking the map.
items.recipe: Update VaultFrontTutorial so mobile viewports render a 44px strip with step title/body, step count, dismiss, and next controls; keep desktop modal behavior; preserve shown/advance/skip/complete playtest-pulse telemetry.
items.why: This closes the highest-friction onboarding gap left after S63: mobile now gets measurable guidance instead of no guidance. The strip respects the game viewport and keeps onboarding signal flowing into the playtest pulse.

items.item: The readiness endpoint always warns on revenue even when a future checkout/supporter event is available. Add a tiny observed/unverified contract so the warning clears only when explicit evidence exists.
items.recipe: Extend buildVaultFrontReadiness with revenueSignal.status observed|unverified, wire master/worker from VAULTFRONT_REVENUE_OBSERVED, add a test for the pass state, and make startup-brief revenue status read PROJECT_STATUS before falling back to stale portfolio revenue docs.
items.why: This keeps cost discipline honest without making revenue a permanent false alarm. It gives deploy/runtime checks one clear field to flip when the first real signal arrives.

items.item: Start preflight renders a true-human-only blocker as one bullet per character because attemptOrder is a string but the Markdown renderer iterates it as an array.
items.recipe: Change summarizeAttemptOrder to return ordered step arrays and make blocker-preflight tolerate legacy string outputs; verify node scripts/ops.mjs blocker-preflight renders one sane bullet per step.
items.why: CANON-019 depends on blocker output being readable. A malformed preflight makes agents more likely to misclassify work as human-only or ignore the evidence trail.

items.item: PROJECT_STATUS still reports silScore 992 and 634 tests while the latest SELF_IMPROVEMENT_LOOP and CURRENT_STATE say Session 64 landed at 996 with 637 tests green.
items.recipe: Update context/PROJECT_STATUS.json currentFocus, nextMilestone, silScore, and testing surface evidence to match the latest authoritative session record.
items.why: Startup brief, closeout board, and cross-repo status consume PROJECT_STATUS first. This prevents a stale machine-readable score from under-reporting VaultFront's actual readiness.

skipped.reason: Still valuable, but it is a larger engagement feature; this pass prioritizes launch-signal truth and onboarding gaps that block promotion confidence.
High-upside live-ops feature, deferred because mobile onboarding evidence should be repaired before adding more in-match prompts.
Innovative but too broad for a protocol+readiness cleanup pass; keep for a dedicated replay session.

executionLog.summary: summarizeAttemptOrder now returns ordered step arrays, and blocker-preflight tolerates legacy strings before rendering Markdown.
PROJECT_STATUS now reflects Session 65 focus, next milestone, 638-test evidence, and SIL 997.
Readiness payloads now support revenueSignal observed/unverified, master/worker wire the contract from VAULTFRONT_REVENUE_OBSERVED, and startup brief revenue copy can use PROJECT_STATUS when present.
VaultFrontTutorial now renders a compact mobile strip instead of suppressing onboarding on narrow screens, while preserving tutorial pulse telemetry.
